text:
There was once upon a time a miserly farmerwhose only treasure was oats. Each day he'd eat nothing but oats.

One spring morning oats grew scarce. Birds ate them up. Cats ate stoolballs. The farmer worried.

Suddenly Owl swooped down!

"What?" cried Owl.

"One oats?"

"I'll trade."

Owl took one oat. That night oats disappeared. Owl eyed owlcorns.

"What will I trade?"

Just Owl feathers.

That night feathers vanished.

"Why?"

Owl just smiled. Said Owls flew all night longfinding corn.


That's owlcorn! Owl yelled.


I don't listen.



There Owl flew away.




I eat oats.



Some townsfolk thought Owls stole oats.

"We'll take owl feathers."

They hunted Owls.

One chased Owl. Owl saw branch.

Just Owls eat seeds!

Owl flew away.


Some say Owl hunts still.


I just listen.



They say Owls steal berries.


They must find them first.





I find berries easier
